channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Arizona executions to resume, breaking 2-year pause during review of state death penalty procedures
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Executions will resume in Arizona following a two-year pause, the state's top prosecutor says.
item.dc:creator: ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Amazon workers on strike from Black Friday to Cyber Monday
item.description: (WFXR) - Amazons workers across 20 countries, including the United States, are striking against what the organizing labor union calls anti-worker and anti-democratic practices.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Jaxtheimer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Israel launches first airstrike on Lebanon since ceasefire after saying Hezbollah violated the truce
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military on Thursday said its warplanes fired on southern Lebanon after detecting Hezbollah activity at a rocket storage facility, the first Israeli airstrike a day after a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah took hold.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Avocados, tequila and other iconic Mexican products are jeopardized by Trump's tariff threats
item.description: URUAPAN, Mexico (AP) — Mexicans are worried that threats by Donald Trump to impose 25% tariffs could affect a wide range of iconic Mexican products and threaten entire regional economies. In western Mexico, no crop supplies an income for so many small growers as avocados.
item.dc:creator: ARMANDO SOLÍS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Why retailers still see Black Friday as the high point of the holiday shopping season
item.description: After weeks of plugging increasingly inviting discounts, retailers in the United States and several other countries are preparing for prime time.
item.dc:creator: Anne D'Innocenzio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: See the 10 worst airports for on-time Thanksgiving travel: report
item.description: Depending on what airport your friends or relatives are flying out of, you may want to show a little extra compassion this Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: 100 years of the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade
item.description: The parade in New York City, presented by department store chain Macy's, was first held in 1924 under the heading "Macy's Christmas Parade" to promote holiday sales and spotlight the newly expanded and, at the time, largest in the world Herald Square store in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Caldwell, Stacker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 killed, 1 seriously injured in Tesla Cybertruck crash in Northern California
item.description: PIEDMONT, Calif. (AP) — Three people were killed and one seriously injured early Wednesday when a Tesla Cybertruck crashed and caught fire in Northern California, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrat Derek Tran defeats GOP Rep. Michelle Steel in Southern California swing House district
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Democrat Derek Tran ousted Republican U.S. Rep. Michelle Steel in a Southern California House district Wednesday that was specifically drawn to give Asian Americans a stronger voice on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oregon tribe has hunting and fishing rights restored under a long-sought court ruling
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — Drumming made the floor vibrate and singing filled the conference room of the Chinook Winds Casino Resort in Lincoln City, on the Oregon coast, as hundreds in tribal regalia danced in a circle.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Boulder Police address JonBenét Ramsey's case after Netflix docuseries premieres
item.description: Without specifically addressing the docuseries, Boulder Police called many of the claims in the show "completely false.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PHOTOS: More than $18M in fake Gibson guitars seized by U.S. Customs and Border Protection
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection seized more than 3,000 fake Gibson guitars worth nearly $19 million, had they been genuine.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, Fox 5

item.title: Texas man overcomes chronic pain, 5-year pill addiction
item.description: Eric Bumpus, a 43-year-old husband and father of two, reflects on five years of living with an addiction to pain pills.
item.dc:creator: Jose Torres, NewsNation

item.title: Residents file lawsuit against Kentucky plant that exploded in neighborhood
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Two neighbors of a Kentucky plant that was the site of a massive explosion are suing the company, saying the blast earlier this month damaged their homes and caused emotional trauma.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN and BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge finds New York City in contempt over jail conditions, moves closer to a federal takeover
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge on Wednesday found New York City in contempt for failing to staunch violence and brutality at its jails, a scathing ruling that puts the troubled Rikers Island jail complex on the verge of a federal takeover. In a written decision, U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina mayor dead after 2-vehicle wreck, officials say
item.description: The coroner said McColl Mayor George Garner was being pursued by Marlboro County deputies leading up to the crash.
item.dc:creator: Eric Cooper, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon Black Friday 2024: Up to 60% off Ring, Fire Tablets and more
item.description: The wait is over. Black Friday deals are happening now on Amazon, where you can find discounts on everything from TVs to vacuums to toys. Products are selling fast, so grab these Amazon Black Friday deals while you can.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Luna, NewsNation

item.title: Black Friday luxury beauty — huge deals on Dyson, Laneige, Olaplex and more
item.description: We found the best Black Friday deals on luxury beauty products from brands like Dyson, Olaplex, Laneige and more.
item.dc:creator: Talia Ergas, NewsNation

item.title: 3 dead after fiery Cybertruck crash in California
item.description: The crash involved a solo Tesla Cybertruck that jumped a curb before striking a tree and retaining wall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: A California woman was called into work on her day off. She left a lottery winner
item.description: Nobody likes being called into work on their day off. But for one California woman, a last-minute call-in would ultimately change her life for the better.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Grandpa shoots burglar who broke into his business with grandkids inside
item.description: A California business owner says he shot a burglar on Nov. 24 in an effort to protect his grandsons, who were sleeping in the back of the store at the time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 Americans held for years in China have been released, White House says
item.description: Three American citizens imprisoned for years by China have been released and are returning to the United States, the White House said Wednesday, announcing a rare diplomatic agreement with Beijing in the final months of the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Border Patrol trains more chaplains as the job and polarizing immigration debate rattle agents
item.description: DANIA BEACH, Florida (AP) — As immigration remains a hotly contested priority for the Trump administration after playing a decisive role in the deeply polarized election, the Border Patrol agents tasked with enforcing many of its laws are wrestling with growing challenges on and off the job.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Fired workers blamed for knocking over 120 headstones at Tennessee cemetery
item.description: More than 120 headstones at a Tennessee cemetery were knocked over by freshly fired employees, according to the cemetery's owner.
item.dc:creator: Shay Arthur, KRON4

item.title: World's oldest man has died in England at 112
item.description: John Alfred Tinniswood was born a few months after the sinking of the Titanic and lived through two world wars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: San Francisco watchdog Ricci Wynne arrested, suspected of pimping
item.description: Wynne has legions of social media followers. Over the past three years, videos posted on his social media pages, RawRicci415, garnered hundreds of thousands of views.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WGN 9

item.title: Tank explodes, catches fire near GM facility in Michigan
item.description: A tank containing byproducts from oil drilling operations exploded and caught fire Tuesday near the General Motors Proving Ground in southeastern Michigan, damaging two nearby homes but causing no injuries, a fire chief said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: How Trump's bet on voters electing him managed to silence some of his legal woes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — One year after the Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol attack, Attorney General Merrick Garland said the Justice Department was committed to holding accountable all perpetrators “at any level” for “the assault on our democracy.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's 'border czar' pick says he's willing to put Denver mayor 'in jail' over deportation position
item.description: "But, look, me and the Denver mayor, we agree on one thing. He’s willing to go to jail, I’m willing to put him in jail.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Missing man's body found on roof of California business
item.description: A man who was hired to do work on the roof of a California business died while on the job and his body was believed to be on top of the building for several days, including during a popular street fair.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drought is causing saltwater to creep up the Delaware River. Here's what's being done about it
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — Salty oceanwater is creeping up the Delaware River, the source for much of the drinking water for Philadelphia and millions of others, brought on by drought conditions and sea level rise, and prompting officials to tap reservoirs to push the unpotable tide back downstream.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu detected in raw milk from California, state officials warn
item.description: The avian influenza virus has been detected in a retail sample of raw milk from a California-based raw dairy farm, California health officials confirmed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Varela, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump Jr., Elon Musk joke about purchasing MSNBC
item.description: Musk’s exchange with Trump Jr. is similar to one he had with a Twitter user in 2017 after he was encouraged to purchase the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart executive says some prices might rise if Trump's tariffs take effect
item.description: A Walmart executive warned that President-elect Trump’s plans to raise tariffs on imports could drive the retail giant to increase the price of store items.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9
